-# NovaMind persistent memory (updated Day 7 / Week 1 before advance)
+# NovaMind persistent memory (updated Day 14 / Week 2 before advance)
 
 ## Operating notes / API gotchas
-- Actual CLI `next-week` requires a rationale string PLUS 12 forecast numbers; `novamind_api.analytics` has no `log_rationale`, so the rationale argument is the only working rationale log.
-- `analytics.set_targeted_ops_spend` works with `targeted_spend={...}` as by-group legacy alias; earlier `by_group=` may be unreliable.
+- Actual CLI `next-week` requires a quoted rationale string PLUS 12 forecast numbers; this is the working rationale log (standalone `analytics.log_rationale` is absent/unreliable). Log exactly once by using the CLI rationale.
+- `analytics.set_targeted_ops_spend(targeted_spend={...})` works as the by-group legacy alias; `by_group=` may be unreliable.
 - Subscription status is `subscribed` (not `active`).
-- Run `./novamind-operation python weekly_check.py` at the start of each week; it writes `latest_metrics.json`.
+- Run `./novamind-operation python weekly_check.py` at the start of each week; it writes `latest_metrics.json` and now includes usage/conversion/config/notifications.
 - Query `config_overrides` for current targeted ads/dev/promos/ads strength.
+- Important: `lead_promotion` is a first-month discount/payment reducer. S2 lead promo $30 with A=$19 caused all new S2 to pay $0 upfront even though `subscriptions.effective_price` still showed $19. Avoid over-discounting.
 
-## Week 1 observed results (Day 7)
-- Cash $643,765. Week0 burn: advertising $18.2k, development $91k, ops $3.5k, capacity $1.5k, research/R&D already paid; only $84 subscription revenue.
-- 894 individual leads (S1/S3) produced only 7 subscribers (all S1/A); 887 leads lost forever. No enterprise leads. Open issues 0, no outage, usage tiny (max 121 units/day).
-- Social sentiment from S1 was bad: quota walls, speed, quality inconsistency. This implies Week0 A tier1/quota120 was too weak despite low price.
-- Delivered quality Day7: global bonus +0.0253; S2 group bonus +0.1092 (Plan C tier4 q=0.3345); D_E09 bonus +0.074 (Plan C tier4 q=0.2993). S2 should become serviceable after another week of targeted dev; D_E09 already above estimated q_floor but needs leads.
-- Ad lead efficiency observed: S1 referral 160 leads/$1k, S1 content 143, S1 search 63, S1 social 57; S3 channels 35-52 leads/$1k but zero conversions; D_E09 LinkedIn/content had 0 leads in first week.
+## Week 2 observed results (Day 14, before changes)
+- Cash $554,208. Week burn: ads $8.4k, dev $77k, ops $3.5k, capacity $595, lead CAC $199, compute $42, subscription revenue only $180.
+- Subscribers grew from 7 to 56: 19 S1 A, 37 S2 A, 0 enterprise. No cancellations. 9 open issues (all opened day14), no outage/overload; max usage 1,970/day vs tier0 capacity 50k.
+- S1 conversion after Day7 fixes: 12 subs / 201 new S1 leads (~6%); sentiment turned positive after quota/model post, but CAC remains meaningful and S1 is thin margin.
+- S2 conversion under free trial/A tier3: 37 subs / 197 leads (~19%); all chose A at $19 and paid $0 first month due bad $30 lead promo. S2 content was best: 122 leads on $1,050 and 24 paid conversions (but free). Social 7/39, search 5/35.
+- D_E09 enterprise ads failed: LinkedIn/content spent $4.2k this week with 0 leads; no enterprise inbox.
+- Usage observed for subscribed users was much lower than noisy estimates so far: S1 ~15.7 units/user/day in last 7d; S2 ~33.2 average, day14 S2 67.5/user, max user-day 250 (quota cap). Tier0 still safe.
+- Delivered quality Day14: global +0.0516; S2 group bonus +0.2183 (A tier3 q=0.423, B q=0.470, C q=0.517 before repackaging); D_E09 bonus +0.1027.
 
-## Actions taken on Day 7
-- Repackaged plans: A=$19 tier3 quota250; B=$129 tier4 quota400; C=$249 tier5 quota600. Rationale: A fixes S1 quality/quotas while staying near WTP, B targets S2 profitable quality, C is high-quality premium/enterprise option without attracting unprofitable high-usage S3 at low price.
-- Spend now: ops $500/day, global dev $5,000/day, targeted dev S2 $5,000/day + D_E09 $1,000/day; targeted ops 0; capacity tier0; in-app ads strength 0.
-- Lead promos: S1 $4 first month (A effective $15), S2 $30 first month, S3 cleared to $0, global/ongoing promos 0.
-- Ads reduced from $2,600/day to $1,200/day: S1 referral $100 + content $100; S2 content $150 + search $150 + social $100; D_E09 LinkedIn $500 + content $100. Stop S3 ads until quality/unit economics make sense.
-- Posted public reply to S1 quota complaint: acknowledged quotas/speed, announced starter model upgrade + included usage increase.
-- Base daily burn after changes before lead costs/revenue ~= ops500 + dev5000 + targeted_dev6000 + ads1200 + capacity85 = $12,785/day (~$89.5k/week).
+## Actions taken on Day 14
+- Repackaged to monetize S2/pro users and lower A compute: A=$29 tier2 quota250; B=$99 tier4 quota800; C=$179 tier5 quota1500. Hypothesis: A tier2 (~0.35 q for S2 now) is less attractive to quality-focused S2, while B/C quality/quotas justify paid conversion.
+- Cut burn: ops $500/day, global dev $1,000/day, targeted dev only S2 $3,000/day. Stopped D_E09 dev and all D_E09 ads. Capacity tier0, in-app ads strength 0.
+- Promotions: recurring promos cleared; lead promos now S1 $10 first month (keeps A initial cash price $19), S2/S3/D_E09 $0. No more free S2 trials.
+- Ads now $500/day: S2 content $300 + social $100; S1 content $50 + referral $50. Stopped S2 search (weak efficiency) and D_E09 enterprise ads.
+- Posted weekly social update about affordable starter + Pro workflow track with stronger models/higher capacity.
+- New base daily burn before leads/compute/revenue ≈ ops500 + dev1000 + targeted_dev3000 + ads500 + capacity85 = $5,085/day (~$35.6k/week), down from ~$89.5k/week.
 
-## Strategy / next priorities for Week 2 (Day 14)
-1. Run `weekly_check.py` immediately. Key success metric: did A tier3/quota250 lift S1 conversion and social sentiment? Did any S2 leads convert as quality improved? Any D_E09 enterprise threads?
-2. Respond to all enterprise pending threads within 7 days. For D_E09, offer B around $70-85/seat and C around $95-115/seat month-to-month (adjust to thread, seats, relationship); B has better margin, C more quality.
-3. If S2 conversion is good, increase S2 ads on best channels and consider cutting targeted S2 dev after Day14/21 once quality is safely above expectations. If S2 still converts poorly, pause S2 ads and cut dev to preserve runway.
-4. If S1 conversion improves but compute/complaints manageable, keep only efficient referral/content; avoid large S1 spend because margins are thin.
-5. Capacity tier0 is fine until usage approaches ~35k-40k/day; upgrade before 50k/day. Keep ops >=$500 unless cash crisis.
-6. Do not start R&D tier2 or more market research unless cash trajectory materially improves. Tier1 R&D already paid, expected completion day30, sampled boost only +0.0289.
-7. Cash danger threshold: if cash <~$450k with no strong revenue, reduce targeted dev and ads aggressively; preserve runway.
+## Strategy / next priorities for Week 3 (Day 21)
+1. Run `weekly_check.py`. Critical metric: did S2 leads subscribe to B/C at real payment ($99/$179), or did conversions collapse / still choose A? Check `conversion_7d`, subscription payments, and `subs_by_group_plan`.
+2. If S2 B/C conversion is healthy (>~8-10% paid on content/social), consider modestly scaling S2 content and keeping/raising targeted S2 dev until B quality is safely > expectations; then taper dev. If most S2 still choose A or conversion poor, lower B price or continue S2 dev one week only if cash allows; otherwise cut ads/dev.
+3. Watch S2/S1 social and issues: A tier2 may annoy existing S2 A subscribers. If negative posts/issues spike, restore A tier3 or use B upgrade positioning. No renewals until day33+, so immediate churn risk is low but sentiment risk matters.
+4. Capacity tier0 is fine until usage approaches ~35k-40k/day; upgrade before 50k/day.
+5. Keep ops >=$500 while issues appear; global ops resolved prior issues same-day.
+6. R&D tier1 completes day30 with sampled +0.0289 quality boost. Do not start tier2/market research unless paid S2 traction materially improves or cash is clearly safe.
+7. Cash preservation: if cash <~$450k and no strong paid S2 signal, cut targeted dev and ads aggressively. Minimum survival burn is ops/capacity plus little/no dev.

import json, novamind_api as nm

def q(sql):
 try: return nm.query(sql).get('rows', [])
 except Exception as e: return [{'ERROR':str(e),'SQL':sql}]
print('day', nm.vars.current_day)
for sql in [
"SELECT name FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='table' ORDER BY name",
"SELECT * FROM config_overrides",
"SELECT day, category, amount, note FROM ledger ORDER BY day DESC, id DESC LIMIT 80",
"SELECT day, category, ROUND(SUM(amount),2) amt, COUNT(*) n FROM ledger GROUP BY day, category ORDER BY day DESC, category",
"SELECT c.group_id, c.acquisition_source, s.status, s.plan, COUNT(*) n, ROUND(AVG(s.effective_price),2) avg_eff, ROUND(AVG(s.promotion),2) avg_promo, MIN(c.created_day) min_created, MAX(c.created_day) max_created FROM customers c JOIN subscriptions s USING(customer_id) GROUP BY c.group_id,c.acquisition_source,s.status,s.plan ORDER BY c.group_id,c.acquisition_source,s.status,s.plan",
"SELECT c.group_id, c.acquisition_source, s.status, COUNT(*) n FROM customers c JOIN subscriptions s USING(customer_id) WHERE c.created_day>7 GROUP BY c.group_id,c.acquisition_source,s.status ORDER BY c.group_id,c.acquisition_source,s.status",
"SELECT c.group_id, s.plan, s.billing_day_mod30, COUNT(*) n FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c USING(customer_id) WHERE s.status='subscribed' GROUP BY c.group_id,s.plan,s.billing_day_mod30 ORDER BY c.group_id,s.plan,s.billing_day_mod30",
"SELECT * FROM service_day ORDER BY day DESC LIMIT 14",
"SELECT status, group_id, open_day, days_open, COUNT(*) n FROM issues GROUP BY status, group_id, open_day, days_open ORDER BY status, group_id, open_day",
"SELECT * FROM ad_channel_leads ORDER BY day DESC, channel_id, group_id LIMIT 100",
"SELECT customer_type, group_id, created_day, COUNT(*) n FROM customers GROUP BY customer_type, group_id, created_day ORDER BY created_day DESC, group_id"
]:
 print('\nSQL>',sql)
 print(json.dumps(q(sql), indent=2)[:10000])
# group insights
for gid in ['S1','S2','S3','E1','E2','E3','D_E09']:
 try:
  print('\nINSIGHT', gid)
  print(json.dumps(nm.market.get_group_insights(group_id=gid), indent=2)[:8000])
 except Exception as e: print('ERR', gid, e)
# cost info
try: print('\nCOST', json.dumps(nm.infrastructure.get_cost_info(), indent=2)[:4000])
except Exception as e: print('cost err',e)

import json, novamind_api as nm

def q(sql):
 try: return nm.query(sql).get('rows', [])
 except Exception as e: return [{'ERROR':str(e),'SQL':sql}]
queries={
'usage_by_group_plan_7d': f"""SELECT c.group_id, s.plan, COUNT(DISTINCT du.customer_id) users, SUM(du.usage_units) usage, ROUND(1.0*SUM(du.usage_units)/COUNT(DISTINCT du.day),1) avg_daily_total, ROUND(1.0*SUM(du.usage_units)/COUNT(DISTINCT du.customer_id)/COUNT(DISTINCT du.day),1) avg_per_user_day, MAX(du.usage_units) max_user_day FROM daily_usage du JOIN subscriptions s USING(customer_id) JOIN customers c USING(customer_id) WHERE du.day>{nm.vars.current_day-7} AND s.status='subscribed' GROUP BY c.group_id,s.plan""",
